ontology.foodex2_description: Non-alcoholic beverages (excluding water and fruit/vegetable juices)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code specific to Air Katira. A00FL (non-alcoholic beverages) used as the closest defensible FoodEx2 category anchor.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Air Katira is approximately 300 ml (one full glass), as commonly served at Ramadan bazaars and traditional beverage stalls in Malaysia and Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Milk-based beverages and composite drink nutrient profiles — IMR Malaysia MyFCD and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component estimation
nutrient_source.organization: Institute for Medical Research (IMR) Malaysia — MyFCD; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Air Katira as a composite beverage. Nutrient values are estimated from IMR Malaysia MyFCD entries for fresh milk, evaporated milk, and dates, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for basil seeds and tragacanth gum. Input AI-generated values (confidence 0.9) are retained where consistent with component-level estimates. Energy 85 kcal/100g, protein 2.5g, fat 2.8g, carbohydrate 13.5g are plausible for a milk-and-date-based beverage at typical dilution. All values documented as estimated.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — milk-based beverages and dates as analogous components
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Air Katira in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 52 (low) based on analogous components: full-fat milk (GI ~27, University of Sydney), dates (GI ~42–62, University of Sydney), and basil/tragacanth gum fibre which moderates postprandial glucose response. Weighted composite estimate yields approximately GI 52. Glycemic load per 300g serving = (52 × 13.5g carbohydrate × 3) / 100 = 21.1g. Confidence: medium.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (45 mg/100g), high potassium (120 mg/100g), and calcium (85 mg/100g) content
hypertension.guidance: Air Katira is low in sodium and provides potassium and calcium, which support blood pressure regulation; individuals with hypertension may consume a standard 300 ml serving without significant concern, provided no additional salt or high-sodium syrups are added.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate sugar content (11.2g/100g) and glycemic load of approximately 21g per 300ml serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the GI is estimated low (~52) due to fibre from tragacanth gum and basil seeds and fat and protein from milk, the total glycemic load per standard 300ml serving is approximately 21g, which is moderate; individuals with type 2 diabetes should limit portion size to 150–200ml and choose preparations with reduced added sugar or syrup.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~1.6g/100g), moderate dietary fibre (1.5g/100g), and low sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Air Katira contains modest saturated fat from milk and is low in sodium; the soluble fibre from tragacanth gum and basil seeds may modestly support lipid management, making a 300ml serving acceptable for most individuals with cardiovascular disease when consumed without excessive added sugar.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Potentially anti-inflammatory ingredients (basil seeds, dates) but moderate sugar content
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The fibre-rich and antioxidant-containing ingredients in Air Katira may be compatible with a rheumatoid arthritis diet; however, individuals should monitor sugar intake as high sugar consumption may exacerbate systemic inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Air Katira (also known simply as Katira or Minuman Katira) is a traditional Malay cooling beverage widely consumed in Malaysia and Singapore, particularly during the Ramadan fasting month as an iftar drink. The drink is prepared by soaking tragacanth gum (getah katira) in water until it swells into a translucent, jelly-like mass, then combining it with basil seeds (biji selasih), malva nuts (kembang semangkuk), chopped dates, fresh milk, evaporated milk, pandan-infused water, and sugar syrup, and serving the mixture chilled over ice. The resulting beverage is creamy, mildly sweet, and has a distinctive multi-textured mouthfeel from the swollen gum granules, slippery basil seeds, and soft date pieces. The colour ranges from pale green (pandan-tinted) to light brown (date-tinted). Nutritionally, a 300ml serving provides approximately 255 kcal, 7.5g protein, 8.4g fat, and 40.5g carbohydrate, with meaningful calcium (255mg), potassium (360mg), and dietary fibre (4.5g) from its composite ingredients. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 52 (low) due to the moderating effects of soluble fibre from tragacanth gum and basil seeds and the fat and protein from milk. Despite the low GI, the glycemic load per serving is moderate (~21g), so individuals with type 2 diabetes should limit portion size. The beverage is halal, vegetarian, gluten-free, and contains milk as the primary allergen. It is culturally significant as a Ramadan rehydration and energy-replenishment drink and is commonly sold at Ramadan bazaars across Southeast Asia.

What are the ingredients in Air Katira?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Air Katira as a composite beverage. Nutrient values are estimated from IMR Malaysia MyFCD and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component-level data for milk, dates, and basil seeds, cross-validated against the AI-generated input values (confidence 0.9). The GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component data. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach and absence of a direct laboratory analysis of this specific beverage.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-level estimates derived from IMR Malaysia MyFCD (fresh milk, evaporated milk, dates) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (basil seeds, tragacanth gum proxy). No direct laboratory analysis of Air Katira as a composite beverage was found in any approved source. Input AI-generated values retained where consistent with component estimates. 2. water_g set to -1: moisture content of the final composite beverage as prepared (with ice dilution) is not reported in IMR MyFCD or USDA FoodData Central for this specific preparation. 3. GI is an analogous estimate (~52, low) based on University of Sydney GI Database values for full-fat milk (GI ~27) and dates (GI ~42–62); tragacanth gum and basil seed fibre are expected to moderate postprandial glucose response. 4. saturated_fat_g (1.6g), monounsaturated_fat_g (0.7g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.1g), and cholesterol_mg (8.0mg) are estimated from milk component data in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (whole milk, NDB 01077) and are approximate. 5. All three core macros (protein_g 2.5, fat_g 2.8, carbohydrate_g 13.5) are non-zero and consistent with a milk-and-date-based beverage at typical dilution; no zero-macro justification required. 6. This food is classified as Malay cuisine but is also widely consumed in Singapore, which triggers the Singaporean multilingual naming requirements. Therefore, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Tamil, and Hindi local names have been added.
